blurb_en: A Chinese tech company called Geedge Networks has been building an AI surveillance system to identify who is likely to become a political dissident before they've done anything. Documents reviewed by Vanderbilt University researchers show the system ingests telecom records, location data, and social media to generate behavioral profiles on individuals flagged as future threats. The key word is future. China's predictive policing tools have existed for years, but adding modern AI closes the gap between what you've done and what a model thinks you might do. If the technology reaches operational use, the category of political threat expands from people who have spoken out to people a model has pre-scored as likely to -- no action required.
blurb_zh: 中国科技公司极迪网络（Geedge Networks）一直在开发一套AI监控系统，用于识别未来可能成为异见人士的公民——此时他们尚未采取任何行动。范德堡大学研究人员审查的内部文件显示，该系统整合电话记录、位置数据与社交媒体，对被标记为潜在风险的个人建立行为画像。关键词是潜在。中国预测性执法工具由来已久，但与现代AI的结合消弭了已做之事与算法预判你可能之事之间的界限。一旦这项技术投入使用，政治威胁的定义将从发声的人扩展到算法认为可能发声的人——无需任何实际行动。
verification_notes: Business Standard (June 1), Political Wire, Newsmax, France 24 (June 3), and ResetEra all independently cite the same Vanderbilt University research on Geedge Networks. ResetEra credits the New York Times as the original report. Four independent secondary sources confirm consistent facts: company name, technology type, researcher affiliation, and capabilities described.

blurb_en: President Trump signed an executive order on June 2 creating a voluntary framework for AI companies to submit frontier models to the government for review up to 30 days before public release. The final version was substantially diluted from early drafts: an initial proposal for a 90-day review window was cut to 30 after pushback led by former White House AI czar David Sacks, with AI companies and investors arguing a longer window would hand China a competitive advantage. The word voluntary appears prominently -- no company is required to participate. The order also mandates federal agencies to build AI cybersecurity benchmarks and establish an AI cybersecurity clearinghouse. After months of delays, the administration has committed to a framework that gave industry most of what it asked for while giving regulators something to point to.
blurb_zh: 6月2日，特朗普签署行政令，要求AI公司在向公众发布前沿模型前，自愿接受政府为期30天的审查。最终版本与早期草案相比大幅缩水：原定90天的审查期，在以前白宫AI顾问大卫·萨克斯为首的科技行业施压下被压缩至30天——理由是更长的审查期将把竞争优势拱手让给中国。自愿二字是关键：没有公司被强制要求参与。行政令还要求联邦机构建立AI网络安全基准，并设立AI网络安全交流中心。历经数月推迟与反复修改，这份行政令最终给了科技行业想要的大部分条件，同时也给监管者留下了一张可以展示的名片。
verification_notes: The Register, NPR, PBS NewsHour, TechCrunch, and Deadline all independently report the June 2 signing. White House official URL confirmed at whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2026/06/. Distinct from 2026-05-21-white-house-ai-eo-postponed (the signed version has different content: 90 days reduced to 30, voluntary nature confirmed, new cybersecurity clearinghouse mandate added).

blurb_en: Microsoft introduced Scout at Build 2026 -- a persistent AI agent that runs continuously across Microsoft 365, Windows, and the web, acting on your behalf without requiring you to open a chat window. Unlike conversational copilots that wait for prompts, Scout is what Microsoft calls an Autopilot: an always-on agent with its own identity that reads emails, fills forms, tracks tasks, and executes multi-step workflows even when you're not looking. It marks a deliberate shift in how Microsoft is framing AI -- not as a tool you invoke but as a permanent presence in your digital environment. Scout is in preview through the Frontier program on Windows 11 and macOS 12.
blurb_zh: 微软在Build 2026大会上发布Scout——一个持续运行于Microsoft 365、Windows和网页之间的自主AI代理，无需你打开聊天窗口，它就已在为你工作。与需要提示词才能激活的对话式AI不同，Scout是微软所谓的自动驾驶（Autopilot）：一个拥有独立身份的常驻代理，可以自主读取邮件、填写表单、跟踪任务并处理多步骤工作流，全程无需人工介入。这代表着微软对AI定位的刻意转变——不再是你调用的工具，而是永久存在于你数字环境中的代理。Scout目前通过Frontier项目在Windows 11和macOS 12上开放预览。
verification_notes: Windows News AI, Tom's Guide Build 2026 live blog, msftnewsnow.com, and Redmond Magazine all independently describe Microsoft Scout with consistent details. Distinct from the existing story 2026-06-02-microsoft-project-polaris-copilot-openai (developer tooling / OpenAI independence); Scout is a consumer/enterprise persistent agent product announced at the same Build 2026 event.
